title: Why this ICU nurse is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support trauma recovery
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Erin Daniel, a 25-year-old ICU nurse from Mansfield, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Daniel is part of Brigham and Women's Hospital's Stepping Strong team, supporting trauma recovery efforts.
Her participation honors Gillian Reny's story and aims to advance trauma research and patient care.

title: Poll: Mass. Legislature underwater in court of public opinion
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A poll commissioned by the Pioneer Institute shows only 28% of Massachusetts residents view the state Legislature favorably.
87% of respondents deem government transparency crucial, while 86% support making lawmakers subject to public records law.
83% of survey participants favor a state audit of the Legislature, reflecting widespread demand for accountability.

title: Police seek Mass. vehicle after driver involved in fatal Providence hit-and-run
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Providence police seek a black Chevrolet Equinox with alloy wheels and a Massachusetts plate in a fatal hit-and-run.
Kevin Chappie, 46, was struck and killed while entering a crosswalk on Corliss Street at 2:35 a.m. Saturday.
Authorities request public assistance and shared contact numbers for tips about the incident involving the Massachusetts-registered vehicle.

title: Martha Stewart’s The Bedford is now open at this New England resort
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Martha Stewart's The Bedford restaurant opened at Foxwoods Resort Casino in Connecticut on March 14.
The Bedford's menu includes Martha Stewart favorites like a $23 bread basket and pierogies, plus exclusive dishes.
The restaurant spans 10,000 square feet, seats 204, and offers breakfast and dinner daily, with lunch on weekends.

title: Weekend commuter rail disruption will now leak into Fridays
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MBTA and Keolis will extend weekend commuter rail shutdowns into Fridays for signal upgrades at North Station.
Travel disruptions will occur on March 20-22, March 27-29, April 10-12, and April 24-27.
Shuttle buses and other lines will replace rail services, adding up to 70 minutes to travel times.

title: This Massachusetts commute ranks as the most stressful, poll finds
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: A poll by Munley Law ranked Zakim Bridge/I-93 North in Boston as Massachusetts's most stressful commute.
The Southeast Expressway and I-95/Route 128 in Waltham follow as the second and third most frustrating drives in Massachusetts.
Boston's Zakim Bridge/I-93 North also ranked seventh nationally among the most dreaded commutes by U.S. drivers.

title: Jaylen Brown describes ‘surreal’ experience at his first St. Patrick’s Day parade in South Boston
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown attended his first St. Patrick's Day parade in South Boston, streaming the experience on Twitch.
Brown, a Boston Celtics guard, enjoyed fan interaction, comparing parade chants to MVP chants at TD Garden.
Brown is having a career year with averages of 28.4 points, 7.1 rebounds, and 5.1 assists, helping the Celtics' playoff position.

title: Suspected mortar shell brought into Essex police station prompts evacuation
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A suspected mortar shell prompted an evacuation at Essex Police Department, later found to be a harmless empty canister.
A resident brought the device into the Essex Public Safety Building after finding it while metal detecting on their property.
Authorities advise the public not to move suspected explosives and to contact 911 if such items are found.

title: Snag one-way flights for as low as $59 on Southwest
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Southwest Airlines offers a three-day sale with one-way flights as low as $59, ending March 19.
Discounted fares apply to travel between April 7 and June 10, for midweek flights only.
Flights from Boston include $59 to Baltimore and $160 to San Diego; book at southwest.com.

title: City councilors call on Boston police to release footage of fatal shooting
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Councilors urge police to release bodycam footage of Stephenson King's fatal shooting in Roxbury.
King, suspected of carjacking, was shot by police after allegedly striking a cruiser on March 11.
Councilors stress transparency and accountability, highlighting the importance of public trust in ongoing investigation.
